description: Output of the Notes Generator pipeline. Triggered when user selects a topic from the syllabus.

input.subject: string
syllabus_context: adjacent topic names for LLM context

contract.topic_id: string (UUID — links back to syllabus topic)
contract.subject: string

sections.heading: string
sections.content: string (markdown allowed — paragraphs, bold, inline code)

contract.summary: string — 2-3 sentence recap of the entire topic
related_topics: string — topic names from the same syllabus

example.subject: Heat Transfer

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Fourier's Law is the fundamental relationship governing **heat conduction** in solids. It states that the rate of heat transfer through a material is proportional to the negative gradient of temperature and the area through which heat flows.
key_points: Heat flows from high temperature to low temperature regions
Rate of conduction depends on material's thermal conductivity (k)

sections.heading: Thermal Conductivity
sections.content: Thermal conductivity `k` is a material property measured in W/m·K. Metals have high k values (copper ~400 W/m·K), while insulators have low values (glass wool ~0.04 W/m·K).
key_points: k is temperature-dependent in real materials
For most problems assume k is constant

example.summary: Fourier's Law defines heat conduction rate as proportional to temperature gradient and cross-sectional area. Thermal conductivity k is the key material property. This law forms the basis for all steady-state conduction problems.

validation_rules: sections array must have at least 2 items
every section must have heading and content
key_points must have 2–5 items per section
formula must be valid LaTeX or null (never empty string)
related_topics must reference real topic names from the syllabus
